title: The Memory Problem, Baseten | Compile 26
summary: Baseten's research team presents an approach to compress the key-value cache for long-horizon agents, enabling near-lossless retrieval of relevant context during inference. The method uses iterative compaction and relates compressed caches to multilayer perceptrons.
writeup: Baseten's research team presents a method to compress the key-value cache for long-horizon agents, aiming to preserve near-lossless retrieval of relevant context during inference. The approach relies on iterative compaction and frames compressed caches in relation to multilayer perceptrons. The work addresses the memory cost of long-context reasoning, where growing KV caches strain test-time compute, and it connects to sparse attention as a way to manage that load.
